source_pr.title: executor: rewrite TestNotFillCacheFlag to avoid data race in CI
source_pr.body:
The old test use a user-defined client to check request, but data race is detected in  `checkRequestClient.notFillCache`
`checkRequestClient` is a global instance used by all goroutine, that's bad.

I rewrite this test case to use `CheckSelectRequestHook` so the CI could be more stable.

@XuHuaiyu @winkyao

symptoms: executor: rewrite TestNotFillCacheFlag to avoid data race in CI
failure_signature: executor: rewrite TestNotFillCacheFlag to avoid data race in CI
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to a data race in the test harness: the checkRequestClient's shared mutable fields (e.g., expectations for NotFillCache) could be read/written concurrently while requests were processed. The patch stabilizes it by rewriting the test to assert expectations via a per-query context hook, avoiding shared mutable state across goroutines.
fix_pattern: Avoid shared mutable state in tests; pass expectations via per-query context/hook so concurrent request handling cannot race.

evidence: evidence: executor: rewrite TestNotFillCacheFlag to avoid data race in CI
